LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANT PATENAUDE & FELIX, APC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MAY 30, 2014 TO GIVE THE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN APRIL 30, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CROSS DEFENDANT PATENAUDE & FELIX, APC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT FAILED TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF ITS MOVING PAPERS AS REQUIRED BY LOCAL RULE 2.6B AND THE COURT'S MINUTE ORDER ISSUED ON APRIL 30, 2014. THE PARTIES ARE ADMONISHED TO COMPLY WITH THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: PAULA SHI #13358.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER NORBERG'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL FILE HIS PROPOSED CROSS-COMPLAINT WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. PURSUANT TO CCP 426.50, THE COURT SHALL LIBERALLY PERMIT COMPULSORY CROSS-COMPLAINTS TO BE FILED, AS LONG AS THE DEFENDANT ACTED IN GOOD FAITH. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT ACT IN GOOD FAITH. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED ACTUAL PREJUDICE. THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR JUNE 2014, WHICH PROVIDES SUFFICIENT TIME FOR DISCOVERY PERTAINING TO THE TWO CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE PROPOSED CROSS-COMPLAINT TO BE CONDUCTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON AUG-01-2014 IN 504, CROSS-COMPLAINANT CHRISTOPHER NORBERG'S MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT INCLUDES HOURS SPENT ON THE REPLY, SUBTRACTS HOURS SPENT ON THE 128.7 MOTION, AND REDUCES THE REMAINING HOURS BY 25% REFLECTING AN ALLOCATION FOR TIME SPENT DEFENDING THE PLAINTIFF'S CASE--RECOGNIZING THAT MOST OF THE EFFORTS WERE DIRECTED TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT AND THAT SOME OF THE OTHER TIME WAS NONETHELESS USEFUL TO THE PROSECUTION OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT--RESULTING IN 69.75 HOURS REASONABLY SPENT. AT AN HOURLY RATE OF $350, WHICH THE COURT CONCLUDES IS REASONABLE, THE COURT AWARDS $24,412.50 IN ATTORNEY FEES. ORDER AND AMENDED JUDGMENT ARE SIGNED. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED
ORDER GRANTING NORBERG'S MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING AMENDED JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF NATIONAL COLLEGIATE STUDENT LOAN TRUST 2007-3, A DELAWARE STATUTORY TRUST (S) TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT NORBERG, CHRISTOPHER ON THE COMPLAINT, SAID DEFENDANT TO HAVE JUDGMENT THEREON FOR $1,331.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $1,331.00, CROSS-DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER NORBERG SHALL RECOVER $200 IN STATUTORY DAMAGES, $241 IN COSTS AND $24,412.50 IN ATTORNEY FEES AGAINST CROSS DEFENDANT PATENAUDE & FELIX, APC. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

MINI MINUTES: CROSS-COMPLAINANT CHRISTOPHER NORBERG'S MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The Court includes hours spent on the reply, subtracts hours spent on the 128.7 motion, and reduces the remaining hours by 25% reflecting an allocation for time spent defending the plaintiff's case--recognizing that most of the efforts were directed to the cross-complaint and that some of the other time was nonetheless useful to the prosecution of the cross-complaint--resulting in 69.75 hours reasonably spent. At an hourly rate of $350, which the Court concludes is reasonable, the Court awards $24,412.50 in attorney fees. Order and amended judgment are signed. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-26-2013 Set For Trial On Jun-09-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File X-Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-22-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JAMES MICHEL, ESQ., 415.239.0156, FOR DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER NORBERG; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER NORBERG'S Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint IS Granted. Defendant shall file his proposed Cross-Complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. Pursuant to CCP 426.50, the Court shall liberally permit compulsory cross-complaints to be filed, as long as the defendant acted in good faith. There is no evidence that Defendant did not act in good faith. Further, Plaintiff has not established actual prejudice. The trial date is set for June 2014, which provides sufficient time for discovery pertaining to the two causes of action in the proposed cross-complaint to be conducted. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
